event.title: Neil deGrasse Tyson An Astrophysicist Goes to the Movies – Part III
event.description: An entertaining and enlightening review of all the science that our favorite movies got wrong, combined with some of the stuff they got right. Incorporates the latest films as well as some classics that you may not have known had any science in them at all.

event.title: Malcolm X and Redd Foxx Washing Dishes In Jimmy's Chicken Shack in Harlem
event.description: In the summer of 1943, two young men—Little & Foxy—form an unlikely bond over fried chicken and a grimy dishwasher. As the world erupts in chaos, their friendship is tested by betrayal, ambition, and history. Inspired by a true story, this world premiere shapes legends.

event.description: The most Tony Award-winning show of 2024. The most Tony Award-nominated play of all time. Stereophonic mines the agony and the ecstasy of creation as it zooms in on a music studio in 1976.

event.title: URINETOWN THE MUSICAL
event.description: Winner of three Tony Awards, Urinetown is a sharp musical satire set in a drought-stricken future where citizens must pay to pee. When one idealist sparks a bathroom rebellion, hilarity erupts—skewering corruption, capitalism, and the cost of survival.

event.title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
event.description: In this new stage adaptation of Agatha Christie’s novel, the village of King’s Abbot is rocked by scandal when the richest man in town, is found dead after the suicide of his fiancée.

event.title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd adapted from the novel by Agatha Christie by Blake Hackler
event.description:
Directed by Jeffrey Schmidt

In this new adaptation, King’s Abbot is shaken when Roger Ackroyd is murdered after his fiancée's suicide. Poirot investigates in this thrilling mystery.

event.title: LOS NIÑOS SANTOS DE MARIA SABINA By Cora Cardona
event.description: Celebrates the life and work of María Sabina, famed Oaxacan doctor and curandera who cured generations of seekers with the powers of psychedelic mushrooms. “I have been a sage since the womb” (“Soy sabia desde el vientre mismo de mi madre”).

event.title: Summer Singing Camp
event.description: GDCS’s free Summer Singing Camp is a weeklong, half-day program for children grades 3–7 that builds musicianship, confidence, and community through joyful choral singing. The camp concludes with a free performance for families and the community.
event.presenter: Greater Dallas Choral Society for Children and Youth (GDCS)

event.title: Como Agua Para Chocolate
event.description: In 1910 Mexico, Pedro Muzquiz and Tita de la Garza fall in love, but their love is forbidden. When Pedro and his father ask Tita’s mother for her hand in marriage, she refuses. In order to stay near Tita, Pedro reluctantly agrees to marry Tita’s older sister, Rosaura.

event.title: MARIACHI
event.description: Through a unique process of collective collaboration, Cara Mía Theatre’s artists are developing a new play about Mariachis living in Texas, spanning their roots in Mexico, and almost a century of carrying the legacy of the Mariachi into the present moment.

event.title: Application Pending
event.description: Uptown Players brings back the hit solo comedy Application Pending, starring BJ Cleveland, August 20–30, 2026 at Theatre Three. This sharp satire skewers Manhattan prep school admissions as Cleveland plays 40+ characters in one frantic, hilarious day.

event.title: Mother Play
event.description: ﻿Uptown Players presents the regional premiere of Paula Vogel’s Mother Play, a darkly funny, semi-autobiographical drama following a single mother and her children through love, loss, and resilience—a piercing look at family, identity, and survival.

event.title: AURORA Biennial 2026
event.description: AURORA Biennial returns from Nov 1-21 with a 3-week, Dallas-wide event series of digital and media art, concluding on Nov 21 with an expansive, one night exhibition across Dallas City Hall, AT&T Discovery District, and beyond.
